pi_items.source: Task description line 2 exactly matches metadata value. This is the specification of what the survey title should be when created.

Task description specifies this exact mask format for Emergency Mobile question. However, official LimeSurvey manual documentation for Short Free Text questions (https://www.limesurvey.org/manual/Question_type_-_Short_free_text) does NOT list 'input_mask' as a supported attribute. Input masking in LimeSurvey typically requires custom JavaScript implementation with jQuery Mask Plugin or Inputmask library, not a native question attribute. The '999' format is a valid mask notation (9=digit placeholder), and the format matches phone masking standards.

Task description specifies this exact mask format for Desk Location Code question (also a Short Free Text). Same limitation as mobile_mask - official LimeSurvey Short Free Text documentation does not document 'input_mask' attribute. Format appears valid for custom masking (2 digits, 2 digits, 3 digits).

LimeSurvey manual documentation (https://manual.limesurvey.org/QS:Min_num_value_n) confirms the attribute is 'min_num_value_n' for numerical input questions. Value 20000 matches task description requirement for Current Annual Salary minimum value.

LimeSurvey manual documentation (https://www.limesurvey.org/manual/Question_type_-_Numerical_input) confirms 'prefix' is a valid attribute for numerical input questions. Example given uses '$' for dollar sign. Task description requires this exact value.
LimeSurvey manual documentation confirms 'suffix' is a valid attribute for numerical input questions. Task description requires this exact value for the salary question.

Task description specifies 'Hide tip' attribute must be enabled on Emergency Mobile question. LimeSurvey manual documentation (https://www.limesurvey.org/manual/QS:Hide_tip) confirms 'hide_tip' is a standard attribute supported on many question types including Short Free Text. Value '1' represents enabled state.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a LimeSurvey 6.x survey titled 'Personnel Data Verification 2025' with specific question configurations. The salary field minimum value is 20000, prefix is '$', and suffix is 'USD' - these are verified as standard LimeSurvey numerical input attributes. The hide_tip attribute enabled on Emergency Mobile is verified as standard. However, the input_mask attributes for the mobile and location code questions (format: (999) 999-9999 and 99-99-999 respectively) are not documented in official LimeSurvey Short Free Text question documentation, suggesting these may be custom/undocumented attributes or require custom JavaScript implementation.
